author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
quote: Die Botschaft hör ich wohl, allein, mir fehlt der Glaube

author: Oscar Wilde
quote: If you are not too long, I will wait here for you all my life.

author: Leo Tolstoy
quote: It is terrible when people do not know God, but it is worse when people identify as God what is not God.

author: Jane Austen
quote: it will, I believe, be everywhere found, that as the clergy are, or are not what they ought to be, so are the rest of the nation.

author: Mark Twain
quote: Be respectful to your superiors, if you have any.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
quote: The only thing of interest in a refuted system is the personal element. It alone is what is forever irrefutable.

author: Maya Angelou
quote: The needs of a society determine its ethics.

author: Virginia Woolf
quote: For our penitence deserves a glimpse only; our toil respite only.

author: Anton Chekhov
quote: An expansive life, one not constrained by four walls, requires as well an expansive pocket.

author: Albert Camus
quote: Like great works, deep feelings always mean more than they are conscious of saying.

author: Confucius
quote: The man of virtue makes the difficulty to be overcome his first business, and success only a subsequent consideration.

author: Kahlil Gibran
quote: Friendship is always a sweet responsibility, never an opportunity.

author: Charles Dickens
quote: …not an orphan in the wide world can be so deserted as the child who is an outcast from a living parent’s love.

author: Rumi
quote: Whoever undertakes a profession without a master becomes the laughingstock of city and town.

author: Henry David Thoreau
quote: What are the earth and all its interests beside the deep surmise which pierces and scatters them?
